article_title: EU Moves to Designate AWS and Azure as DMA Gatekeepers in First Cloud Test of the Digital Markets Act
summary: Accurate, well-sourced report on the EU's preliminary DMA gatekeeper designation of AWS and Azure; every claim, quote, number, name, and date verified verbatim against cited source snapshots. Only finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (750 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis sections are appropriately scoped. Technical and legal nuance (qualitative vs quantitative DMA route) handled accurately and without overstatement.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots read from disk and verified (no re-fetch). source-0 (European Commission, ec.europa.eu, status 200): confirms 'Publication 25 June 2026', 'largest and second largest cloud computing services in the EU respectively', 'an important gateway between businesses and their customers in the EU', 'despite them not meeting the DMA's quantitative thresholds for designation', the Virkkunen quote ('cornerstone of Europe's economy - and a prerequisite for AI'; 'must operate in fair, open and competitive markets that foster trust'), the Ribera comment, and 'now have the opportunity to respond before any final decisions are taken' — all verbatim. source-1 (The Register, status 200): confirms 'both have vast and entrenched user bases and appear to benefit from lock-in effects and high switching costs, in addition to a large ecosystem', 'did not meet the DMA's quantitative thresholds for designation (such as user numbers)', and 'fines of up to 10 percent of worldwide turnover' — verbatim. source-2 (AI Weekly, status 200): confirms 'a first in the regulation's history', 'roughly 65-70% of EU cloud revenue in Q1 2026 by Synergy Research Group data', and 'until September 2026 to submit written representations, with a final Commission decision likely by late October' — verbatim. source-3 (Global Banking & Finance, status 200): confirms the Virkkunen quote, the 'limits on self-preferencing and requirements to ensure interoperability and data portability' obligations, the AWS spokesperson quote ('adding another heavy layer of overlapping regulation under the DMA undermines European competitiveness'), and the Microsoft spokesperson quote ('ignoring the growing power of Google Cloud and Gemini will tilt the market in a harmful way') — verbatim. source-4 (PYMNTS, status 200): confirms 'more than 200 active European cloud providers that have held a roughly 15% share of revenue since 2022' attributed to a Copenhagen Economics study commissioned by AWS — verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabricated docket, threshold, or fine numbers. The 65-70% EU cloud revenue figure is cited only to AI Weekly (single-source, correct — not a compound attribution). The up-to-10%-of-worldwide-turnover fine is cited only to The Register (correct). The 25 June 2026 announcement date, the qualitative-route framing (services NOT meeting quantitative thresholds), the 'gateway'/'thresholds' verbatim phrasing to the EC primary release, the September-2026 written-representations and late-October decision timeline, and the Virkkunen quotes all check out. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Presents both the Commission's reasoning and the companies' pushback (AWS, Microsoft, Copenhagen Economics counter-data). No sensationalism or editorializing; the Analysis section is clearly labeled and stays within sourced facts. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Prior June DMA coverage (02-eu-prepares-record-dma-fine-against-google, 19-italys-agcm-opens-first-national-dma-probe-into-apple) concerns Google search and Apple iCloud respectively — distinct subjects. This is the first Machine Herald coverage of the AWS/Azure cloud gatekeeper designation.
recommendations: Two sources (aiweekly.co, globalbankingandfinance.com) are not on config/source_allowlist.txt. This is a NON-BLOCKING warning; both fetched cleanly at status 200 and their attributed claims verified verbatim. A maintainer may consider adding them to the allowlist, but no allowlist edit is made as part of this review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully verified submission ready for publication. Overriding the script's auto-suggested APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS (triggered solely by the non-blocking allowlist warning) to APPROVE, since there is no factual error, misattribution, or fabrication requiring a public corrections trail.

content_preview.title: EU Moves to Designate AWS and Azure as DMA Gatekeepers in First Cloud Test of the Digital Markets Act
content_preview.summary: The European Commission's preliminary view targets Amazon's and Microsoft's cloud arms, the first DMA designation made without meeting the law's quantitative thresholds.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Commission announced on 25 June 2026 that it had reached a preliminary position that Amazon and Microsoft should be designated as gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act (DMA) for their cloud computing services, Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Microsoft Azure (Azure), according to the [European Commission](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/news/commission-reaches-preliminary-position-amazons-and-microsofts-market-leading-cloud-services-should). If confirmed, it w...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
